content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

August 12, 1976

Jerrold E. Mitchell, Commissioner
and Administrative Officer
Municipal Court
Los Angeles Judicial District
Courthouse, 110 North Grand Ave.
Los Angeles, California 90012

Dear Mr. Mitchell:

In response to your letter of July 30, 1976, and our
telephone conversation of August 11, 1976, please be advised of the following:

Government Code Section 87203 provides that Statements
of Economic Interests shall be filed within 30 days after
the anniversary date of assuming office. Section 87205
provides that: (a) For purposes of determining the anniversary of assuming an office, the date on which the term of
office began is deemed the date of assuming office, whether
or not the person holding the office actually assumed the
office on that date. Therefore, the anniversary date for
a new appointee filling an unexpired term of office would
be the anniversary date of the term of office of his or
her predecessor.

Senate Bill 1356, pending before the Legislature, would
require the Fair Political Practices Commission to set
filing dates for officials required to file pursuant to
Government Code Section 87200. If Senate Bill 1356 is
enacted, the Fair Political Practices Commission will
adopt a regulation requiring judges of courts of record
to file a Statement of Economic Interests during the month
of February. If the bill is not enacted, this office will
be in contact with the clerks of the courts to inform them
of the anniversary dates.


Page Two
August 12, 197[illegible]


In response to your second inquiry, the period covered
by the first Statement of Economic Interests filed by
judges of courts of record will be from May 5, 1976, through
the anniversary date of assuming office.

If you have any further questions, please do not
hesitate to contact this office.

Sincerely,


Dawn Wiser
Conflict of Interest Division


Due to the extremely minimal and illegible content in this image, I cannot provide a meaningful verbatim transcription beyond noting that the page is largely blank.

AREA CODE
213

TELEPHONE
974-6171

MUNICIPAL COURT
LOS ANGELES JUDICIAL DISTRICT
JERROLD E. MITCHELL
COMMISSIONER & ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICER
COURTHOUSE, 110 NORTH GRAND AVENUE
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012

July 30, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

Legislation requiring judges to file a Statement of Economic
Interests became effective on May 5, 1976. Questions have been raised
by the judges of this court regarding their responsibilities under that
legislation. I would, therefore, appreciate your answers to the following,
which I will then disseminate to our judges:

1. What is the anniversary date of a person who has been
appointed by the Governor to fill the unexpired term of a
vacant judicial position, inasmuch as the term of office of
a Municipal Court judge is automatically extended two years
if a person is appointed to an office which became vacant
within ten months prior to the election next preceding the
expiration of the term?

2. Inasmuch as the legislation requiring judges to file a
Statement of Economic Interests became effective on May 5,
1976, is the period covered by a judge's 1977 Statement
the period commencing on his or her anniversary date, or
the period commencing May 5, 1976?

Sincerely,

JERROLD E. MITCHELL, Commissioner
and Administrative Officer

JEM:rs
